condition: When gamma=0, there exists underline p>0 such that p_P(X)>=underline p P_X-almost surely for every law in the class considered for the feasible upper bound.
reason: The frozen gamma=0 marginal-overlap convention does not imply that true propensities are never clipped. A uniform overlap floor is exactly what makes the gamma=0 endpoint reduce to the standard strict-overlap DML/policy-learning bound.
standard_or_novel: standard: strict overlap / uniform positivity, Imbens and Rubin (2015), Athey and Wager (2021)
not_crux: It is not the weak-overlap rate claim; it only identifies the classical gamma=0 endpoint where the true-clipping displacement term is absent.
